title: Transduction of Cultured Cells with Recombinant Lentiviral Particles

steps: Plate cells for transduction
Plate at a density that will reach 80% confluency in ~24hrs (time of transduction).
12-well plate: 16HBE: Plate 1 mL at 5.25 x 10 5 cells/mL
IMR90: Plate 1 mL at 6.4 x 10 4 cells/mL
HULEC: Plate 1 mL at 8 x 10 4 cells/mL
H441: Plate 1 mL at 2.0 x 10 5 cells/mL
NOTE: Plate cell in three wells of a separate multi-well plate to be counted at time of transduction for use in determining the amount of viral supernatant to use for transduction.
NOTE: Plate three additional wells for a no viral transduction control
NOTE: Use collagen coated plates
Incubate cells in a humidified incubator at 37 °C with 5% CO 2 for 24hrs.
Determine Cell Density
Warm appropriate cell culture medium and DPBS in 37°C water bath, and warm trypsin at room temperature
Aspirate cell culture media of designated counting wells
Rinse aspirated wells with DPBS 12-well plate: Add 1 mL DPBS / well
Aspirate PBS from each well and add trypsin. Incubate for 7 minutes at 37 °C 12-well plate: Add 200uL trypsin / well

Neutralize trypsin by the addition of culture media to each well. Triturate each cell suspension to break up cell clumps. Transfer each cell suspension to a separate labeled 15mL tube/well. 12-well plate: Add 1 mL culture media / well
Rinse each well with additional culture media for collection of remaining cells. Transfer to matched 15mL tube. Vortex to mix cell suspension. 12-well plate: Rinse with 1 mL of culture media / well

Add an appropriate amount of DPBS to a new Eppendorf tube for each cell suspension to be counted. Add appropriate amount of each cell suspension to matched Eppendorf tube to make a 1:1 dilution. 12-well plate: Mix 50 μL DPBS with 50 μL cell suspension

Vortex each cell-PBS mix and count via hemocytometer to calculate cell number / well. NOTE: This cell density value will be used to establish the number of cells in the wells designated for transduction.
Transduce cells with recombinant lentivirus

Make stock solution of polybrene Weigh approximately 100 mg of polybrene (it will be challenging to weight exactly 100 mg due to the variable size of polybrene crystals) and add a volume of tissue culture grade water to prepare a stock solution at a concentration of 10 mg/mL.
Filter sterilize in hood and store at 4 °C NOTE: polybrene solution expires one month after preparation.

Make optimized polybrene dilution from 10 mg/mL stock in appropriate amount of growth medium in 15 mL conical tube. Mix well by vortexing. NOTE: The optimal concentration of polybrene was determined by testing dilutions ranging from 0 to 10 μg/mL for maximal increase in viral transduction efficiency. 16HBE: 2 μg/mL IMR90: 2 μg/mL HULEC: 8 μg/mL H441: 2 μg/mL
Thaw aliquot(s) of recombinant lentiviral particles at room temperature

Make aliquots of growth medium with X mg/mL polybrene for each well to be transduced in 50 mL conical tubes. 12-well plate: 800 mL of polybrene dilution / well to be transduced NOTE: Include additional polybrene aliquots to be used as a negative (no virus) transduction control

Add optimized volume of viral particles to each polybrene dilution aliquot designated for viral transduction. Mix well by vortexing. NOTE: To determine optimal concentration of virus to be used for transduction, perform a viral titration assay with serial dilution from 1:400 to 1:12800. Typically, using an MOI of 3, when the viral preparation has been tittered on the target cell type, results in a transduced population that has >90% fluorescent-positive cells.

Remove plates designated for viral transduction from incubator and aspirate media from wells. Aspirate media in a manner that minimizes cell-air contact time Add polybrene-virus mix or polybrene-no virus to appropriate wells 12-well plate: 800 μL of appropriate mix / well
Incubate in a humidified incubator at 37 °C with 5% CO 2 for 24 hrs.
In the morning (~9:00 AM) aspirate the transduction medium into a flask containing bleach.
Rinse wells with DPBS and aspirate the DPBS. Repeat twice for a total of three DPBS washes. 12-well plate: 1mL PBS / well
Add fresh growth medium to each plate of transduced cells. 12-well plate: 1mL of growth media / well
Incubate cells in a humidified incubator at 37 °C with 5% CO 2 for 48 hours
Visualize fluorescent cells and/or quantify the percentage of fluorescent-positive cells by flow cytometry.
No
